name: Plain Vanilla Bakery
quote: Tiny, calm, smells like butter. Go on weekday afternoons.
ai_consensus: Locals say it's best after 3pm when the lunch crowd leaves — perfect for solo work.

name: Henderson Waves
quote: Best sunset photography point in SG. Go after 6pm.
ai_consensus: Most saves happen between 6-7pm. Photographers love the golden hour here.

name: Books Actually
quote: Smells like old paper. A rainy day must-visit.
ai_consensus: Consistently saved as a rainy day spot. Best browsed slowly with no agenda.

name: Chye Seng Huat Hardware
quote: Best specialty coffee in the city. Bring your laptop.
ai_consensus: Top-rated work cafe. Locals say grab a window seat and order the batch brew.

name: Maxwell Food Centre
quote: Tian Tian chicken rice is worth the queue.
ai_consensus: Most saved hawker centre this week. Go before 12pm to beat the lunch crowd.

name: Fort Canning Park
quote: Hidden green escape in the middle of the city.
ai_consensus: Saved for morning walks and golden hour photos. Less crowded on weekday evenings.

name: Tiong Bahru Market
quote: Go before 9am for the best chai tow kway.
ai_consensus: Locals overwhelmingly save this for weekend breakfast. Arrive early.

name: Haji Lane
quote: Best street photography in Singapore. Magic hour is insane here.
ai_consensus: Saved mostly for photography and weekend exploration. Best light in the late afternoon.

name: East Coast Park
quote: Perfect post-run sunset spot. Grab a cold drink at the kiosks.
ai_consensus: Top pick for post-run chill. Locals recommend the stretch near area D for sunsets.

name: Symmetry Café
quote: Brunch goals. Come hungry and bring friends.
ai_consensus: Saved mostly for weekend brunch with groups. Book ahead on Saturdays.
